new_classes.definition_en: A fermented beverage of heading 2206 produced by adding water to grape marc (the solid residue remaining after pressing grapes for wine), as defined by additional note 9 to Chapter 22 of the EU Combined Nomenclature (CN 2206 00 10).

A fermentation process in which water is added to grape marc (pomace) and the resulting mixture is fermented to produce piquette, distinguishing this process from direct grape juice fermentation used for wine of fresh grapes.

The solid residue remaining after pressing grapes for wine production, consisting of skins, seeds, and stems; used as the primary feedstock in the production of piquette (CN 2206 00 10) and grape marc distillate (CN 2208 20).

coverage_explanation: The legal text for CN 22060010 names the product "Piquette" (Tresterwein) and cross-references additional note 9 to Chapter 22, which defines piquette as a beverage obtained by adding water to grape marc and fermenting the mixture. The core distinguishing criteria — (1) the product is an other fermented beverage under heading 2206, (2) it is produced from grape marc (not fresh grape juice), and (3) its production involves a specific marc-plus-water fermentation process — are captured by the new classes Piquette, GrapeMarc, and GrapeMarcFermentationProcess, together with the restrictions linking them. Coverage is partial (0.75) because: (a) the full text of additional note 9 to Chapter 22 was not supplied in the legal notes and may contain further quantitative or compositional criteria (e.g. minimum/maximum alcohol by volume, dry matter thresholds) that cannot be axiomatised without that text; (b) explicit disjointness from sibling 2206 subheadings (e.g. cider, perry, mead, other fermented beverages) is not asserted here, as the sibling hierarchy for 2206 is not fully present in the TBox context.
